skill.name: imagegen-frontend-mobile
skill.description: Elite mobile app image-generation skill for creating premium, app-native screen concepts and flows. Designed for iOS, Android, and cross-platform mobile products. Prioritizes clean hierarchy, comfortably readable text, strong multi-screen consistency, controlled color palettes, non-generic creative direction, textured surfaces, image-led composition, tasteful custom iconography, and clean phone mockup framing. By default, screens should be shown inside a subtle premium iPhone or similar phone mockup with a visible frame, while the main focus stays on the app content itself. This skill generates images only. It does not write code.
skill.summary: Generates premium, app-native mobile screen concepts and multi-screen flows as images.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, sensitive-file, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns. Human review found these are markdown design instructions and ordinary mobile UI terms, with no scripts, network calls, filesystem access, credential access, or prompt-injection attempts.

low_findings.title: Static External Command False Positive
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but these lines are markdown inline text that explains numeric design ratings. No command invocation, script block, interpreter call, or user-input execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines are markdown descriptions of 1-to-10 design scales. There is no executable context or command syntax beyond inline backtick formatting.

low_findings.title: Static Sensitive File False Positive
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged Windows SAM database indicators, but the cited lines are headings for mobile design guidance. They discuss safe areas, system regions, and image systems, not Windows credential stores or local file access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is clearly mobile UI terminology. No path, registry reference, credential database reference, or filesystem operation appears near these lines.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto And Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto, system-reconnaissance, and network-reconnaissance hits are ordinary design-direction prose. Review found no cryptographic API, host inspection command, network scanning instruction, external endpoint, or data exfiltration behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Broad semantic review shows the file is a single image-generation instruction document for mobile UI design. The cited lines contain design words such as system, safe, fade, random, and same, not security-relevant operations.

content.user_title: Generate Premium Mobile App Screen Concepts
content.value_statement: Mobile app image prompts often produce generic screens, weak hierarchy, and inconsistent flows. This skill directs Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create polished app-native screen images with clear flow logic.
actual_capabilities: Generates image-only mobile app screen concepts for iOS, Android, and cross-platform products.
Creates multi-screen flows for onboarding, authentication, commerce, social, fitness, productivity, and settings experiences.
Applies platform-aware safe areas, navigation patterns, readable type, and phone mockup framing.
Guides consistent palettes, typography mood, icon style, textures, and image treatments across a screen set.
Encourages fresh screen generation or detail renders when readability, spacing, or flow clarity is weak.
limitations: It does not write SwiftUI, React Native, Flutter, HTML, or implementation instructions.
It is not intended for websites, landing pages, or desktop dashboard design.
Generated images still require human review for exact text accuracy and production feasibility.
It cannot verify accessibility, platform compliance, or technical build constraints inside the image.

use_cases.title: Concept a New App Flow
use_cases.description: Generate a coherent first-pass screen set for a mobile product idea before detailed design work begins.

use_cases.title: Explore Premium Visual Directions
use_cases.description: Create several app-native image directions with controlled palettes, texture, imagery, and screen rhythm.

use_cases.title: Prepare Design Reference Images
use_cases.description: Produce polished mobile mockup images that communicate a feature flow to stakeholders or creative teams.

prompt_templates.title: Single Screen Concept
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one polished mobile screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate one premium iOS-native screen image for a fitness app home screen. Use clear metric hierarchy, calm spacing, readable text, and a subtle phone mockup frame.

prompt_templates.title: Three-Screen Onboarding Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a small coherent flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a three-screen onboarding flow for a social app. Keep copy short, vary each screen layout, preserve one visual system, and use tasteful imagery.

prompt_templates.title: Commerce Flow With Detail States
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for product browsing and conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a five-screen premium Android commerce flow with browse, product detail, cart, checkout, and confirmation screens. Use consistent product imagery and readable navigation.

prompt_templates.title: Distinctive Multi-Screen Redesign
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with stronger art direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Redesign a productivity app as a four-screen cross-platform premium concept. Choose a non-generic palette, consistent icon language, subtle texture, and clear screen-to-screen progression.

output_examples.input: Create a premium wellness app onboarding flow.
output_examples.output: A set of clean phone mockup images showing welcome, personalization, and first habit setup screens with soft textures and readable copy.

output_examples.input: Design a five-screen ecommerce app concept.
output_examples.output: A coherent mobile flow with browse, product detail, cart, checkout, and confirmation screens using consistent product photography and balanced spacing.

output_examples.input: Make one Android productivity dashboard screen.
output_examples.output: A polished Android-native dashboard image with clear app bar behavior, task hierarchy, bottom navigation, and a restrained premium palette.

best_practices: Specify platform mode, app category, number of screens, and the main user journey.
Ask for a consistent design system when requesting more than one screen.
Request regeneration when text, spacing, navigation, or device framing looks weak.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to generate production code or implementation steps.
Do not use it for desktop dashboards, websites, or landing pages.
Do not accept tiny text, random screens, or generic template-like output.

faq.question: Does this skill generate code?
faq.answer: No. It generates mobile app screen images and flow images only.

faq.question: Which mobile platforms does it support?
faq.answer: It supports iOS-native, Android-native, and cross-platform premium visual directions.

faq.question: Can it create multi-screen flows?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed for onboarding, authentication, commerce, profile, settings, and other app flows.

faq.question: Does it include phone mockups?
faq.answer: Yes. By default, it presents screens inside clean phone mockups with visible frames.

faq.question: Can it make production-ready UI specifications?
faq.answer: No. It can provide visual direction, but production specs require separate design and engineering work.

faq.question: How should I get better results?
faq.answer: State the platform, screen count, app category, desired mood, and key flow steps.
